title: Premium Airport Lounge Face Recognition Entry System - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Build fixed top navigation bar with exactly four links: Live Entry, Member Management, Entry Logs, and Security & System Status with smooth scroll behavior
acceptanceCriteria: Exactly four navigation links are visible
Clicking each link smoothly scrolls to the correct section
Nav bar remains fixed during scroll
No booking, login, or travel-related links are present

file_operations.description: Create a fixed navigation bar component with exactly four links (Live Entry, Member Management, Entry Logs, Security & System Status). Use white/soft-grey background with subtle bottom shadow. Implement smooth scroll navigation to corresponding page sections. Use the authorization component to conditionally show admin-only sections.
Wire the Navigation component into the app layout at the top. Ensure it renders above all page sections and remains fixed during scroll.

requirements.summary: Create full-width Hero Section displaying premium lounge branding with generated background image
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section fills the viewport width
Title and subtitle are clearly readable over the background
Background image is the generated lounge hero asset
Section has a dark gradient overlay

file_operations.description: Create a full-width hero section component with title 'Premium Lounge AI – Face Recognition Entry System', professional subtitle, and background image using frontend/public/assets/generated/lounge-hero.dim_1920x1080.png. Apply dark gradient overlay for text legibility. Use elegant typography with generous whitespace and premium light theme styling (gold/teal accents, soft-grey backgrounds).
Wire the HeroSection component as the first section below the Navigation component. Assign it an ID attribute for smooth scroll navigation.

requirements.summary: Create Face Detection Section with simulated camera feed, bounding-box overlay, and pulsing scan animation
acceptanceCriteria: Card is centered with subtle shadow
Bounding-box frame overlay is visible inside the camera area
Pulsing scan animation runs continuously
Explanatory text is present below or beside the camera card

file_operations.description: Create a face detection section with a centered card containing a simulated camera feed area (static placeholder). Draw a face detection bounding-box frame overlay using CSS borders or SVG. Implement a continuous pulsing scan animation around the frame. Include explanatory text describing the face recognition capture process. Apply premium light theme styling with subtle card shadows.
Wire the FaceDetectionSection component as Section 2 below the Hero. Assign it the ID 'live-entry' for smooth scroll navigation from the nav bar.

requirements.summary: Create AI Processing Section displaying three-step workflow with icons, labels, descriptions, and animated progress indicator
acceptanceCriteria: All three steps are displayed in order
Each step has a distinct icon, label, and description
A progress or connector line/animation links the steps
Layout is clean and horizontally or vertically structured

file_operations.description: Create an AI processing section displaying a three-step workflow: Step 1 – 'Capturing Image', Step 2 – 'Extracting Facial Features', Step 3 – 'Matching Premium Member Database'. Use structured information blocks or a stepper component with distinct icons, labels, and short descriptions for each step. Include a subtle animated progress indicator or connector line between steps. Apply clean, premium light theme styling.
Wire the AIProcessingSection component as Section 3 below the Face Detection Section. Ensure proper spacing and alignment with the overall page layout.

requirements.summary: Create Verification Result Section displaying passenger data, confidence score, and access status with backend integration
acceptanceCriteria: All four data fields are displayed in a clean information panel
Status badge correctly renders green for granted and red for denied
'Run Verification' button triggers the backend simulateVerification call
Result animates into view after the call completes

file_operations.description: Create a React Query mutation hook for calling the backend simulateVerification function. Accept an imageHash parameter and return the VerificationResult including passengerName, membershipTier, confidenceScore, verificationTime, and accessGranted. Handle loading and error states.

Create a verification result section with a 'Run Verification' button that calls the useSimulateVerification hook with a mock image hash. Display an information panel showing Passenger Name, Membership Tier, Confidence Score, and Verification Time. Below the panel, show a large status badge: green 'Access Granted' or red 'Access Denied' based on the accessGranted field. Animate the result reveal after the backend call completes. Apply premium light theme styling with gold/teal accents for granted state and red for denied state.
Wire the VerificationResultSection component as Section 4 below the AI Processing Section. Ensure proper spacing and layout integration.

requirements.summary: Create Access Confirmation Section conditionally styled based on verification result
acceptanceCriteria: Confirmation card displays the required message
Checkmark icon is visible on granted state
Card style changes to a denial state (e.g., red accent, X icon) when access is denied
Section is visually distinct and prominent

file_operations.description: Create an access confirmation section displaying a clean confirmation card with the message 'Access Authorized – Welcome to Premium Lounge' using premium typography. Include a checkmark icon and soft green accent for granted state. Conditionally style the card (confirmation vs. denial state) with red accent and X icon when access is denied. Accept the verification result as a prop from the parent component to determine the state. Apply premium light theme styling with generous whitespace.
Pass the verification result state to the AccessConfirmationSection component so it can conditionally render the appropriate confirmation or denial UI.

Wire the AccessConfirmationSection component as Section 5 below the Verification Result Section or include it within the VerificationResultSection component. Ensure proper integration with the verification flow.

requirements.summary: Create Member Management section with member list and add member form integrated with backend
acceptanceCriteria: Member list renders all stored members with name and tier
Add member form has name and tier fields
Submitting the form calls addMember and refreshes the list
Empty state message is shown when no members exist

file_operations.description: Create a React Query hook for fetching the member list from the backend getMembers function. Return the array of members with id, name, membershipTier, and faceHash fields. Handle loading and error states.

Create a React Query mutation hook for adding a new member via the backend addMember function. Accept name, membershipTier, and a mock faceHash. Invalidate the members query on success to refresh the list.

Create a member management section displaying existing members in a clean table or card grid using the useGetMembers hook. Include an add member form with name and membership tier fields that calls the useAddMember hook on submit. Refresh the list on successful addition. Show an empty state message when no members exist. Use the authorization component to restrict this section to admin users only. Apply premium light theme styling with subtle shadows and elegant typography.

Wire the MemberManagementSection component as a full page section accessible via the navigation bar. Assign it the ID 'member-management' for smooth scroll navigation. Wrap it with authorization guards to ensure only admins can access this section.

requirements.summary: Create Entry Logs section displaying chronological verification attempts integrated with backend
acceptanceCriteria: Entry logs list is displayed in reverse-chronological order
Each entry shows timestamp, name, result, and confidence score
A new log entry is created each time simulateVerification is called
Empty state message is shown when no logs exist

file_operations.description: Create a React Query hook for fetching entry logs from the backend getEntryLogs function. Return the array of EntryLog objects with timestamp, passengerName, result (Granted/Denied), and confidenceScore. Handle loading and error states.

Create an entry logs section displaying a chronological log of verification attempts using the useGetEntryLogs hook. Show each log entry with timestamp (formatted as human-readable date/time), passenger name (or 'Unknown'), result (Granted/Denied), and confidence score. Display in reverse-chronological order. Show an empty state message when no logs exist. Use the authorization component to restrict this section to admin users only. Apply premium light theme styling with clean table or list layout.

Wire the EntryLogsSection component as a full page section accessible via the navigation bar. Assign it the ID 'entry-logs' for smooth scroll navigation. Wrap it with authorization guards to ensure only admins can access this section.
After a successful simulateVerification call, invalidate the entry logs query to automatically refresh the logs list and show the new entry.

requirements.summary: Create Security & System Status section displaying system health dashboard with status indicators
acceptanceCriteria: At least four system status indicators are displayed
Each indicator has a colored status dot (green = OK)
Last System Check shows a realistic timestamp
Layout is clean and dashboard-like

file_operations.description: Create a security & system status section displaying a system health dashboard with static but realistic status indicators: Face Recognition Engine (Online), Database Connection (Secure), Camera Feed (Active), Last System Check (timestamp). Use green/amber/red status dots for each indicator. Generate a realistic timestamp for Last System Check on component mount. Apply premium light theme styling with clean dashboard-like layout and elegant typography.
Wire the SystemStatusSection component as a full page section accessible via the navigation bar. Assign it the ID 'system-status' for smooth scroll navigation.

requirements.summary: Apply consistent premium light theme across the entire application with gold/teal accents, elegant typography, and smooth animations
acceptanceCriteria: All sections use the defined light theme palette
Gold or muted teal accent is consistently applied to CTAs, badges, and highlights
Smooth scroll is active for all nav links
Cards have subtle box shadows
Typography is elegant and hierarchically consistent

file_operations.description: Apply a consistent premium light theme across the entire application: white or soft-grey (#F5F5F7 range) backgrounds, deep charcoal or near-black typography, gold or muted teal as the primary accent color. Configure smooth scroll-behavior. Define CSS custom properties for the premium color palette (no blue/purple). Set up elegant sans-serif typography (Inter or similar) with hierarchical font sizes and weights. Include subtle shadow utilities for cards. Add minimal hover and reveal animation keyframes.

Extend the Tailwind theme to include the premium light theme color palette: soft-grey backgrounds (#F5F5F7), charcoal text, gold and muted teal accent colors. Configure elegant sans-serif font family (Inter or similar). Add custom spacing and shadow utilities for generous whitespace and subtle card shadows. Enable smooth scroll behavior globally.

requirements.summary: Render generated hero background image as a static asset in the Hero Section
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section uses lounge-hero.dim_1920x1080.png as its background image
Image is served from frontend/public/assets/generated
Image covers the full hero area with object-fit: cover

file_operations.description: Reference the generated hero background image at frontend/public/assets/generated/lounge-hero.dim_1920x1080.png as the Hero Section background. Use inline style or CSS background-image property with the full workspace path. Apply object-fit: cover (or background-size: cover) to ensure the image fills the full hero area. Ensure the dark gradient overlay is applied on top of the background image for text legibility.
